title: S-SUBSTITUTED THIOUREAS AS ANALYTICAL REAGENTS. PAR II. S-METHYLTHIOUREA SULPHATE AS A REAGENT FOR SILVER
author: SUSHlL KUMAR SlDDHANTA
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: S-Methylthiourea sulphate has been successfully employed as a gravimetrie reagent for silver. The method takes advantage of the formation of a highly insoluble, stable precipitate of silver methvliner­captide, AgCH 3 S, which coagulates rapidly and may be filtered easily. The precipitation should be done in an amrnoniacaI medium. A quantitative separation of silver from zinc has also been made from a strongly ammoniacal solution containing ammonium nitrate. The precipitate of AgCH 3 S, is less soluble than AgCl.
